canonicalSpecs.purpose: Credential-entry bootstrap transport and shared consumer ownership.
Lifecycle credential policy and public/private environment boundaries.
Runtime configuration and SDK bootstrap rules.
Credential secrecy, token replacement, and fail-closed behavior.
Lifecycle matrix, browser handoff, and static governance verification.

integration.authority: Root SDKWork specs remain authoritative. Local specs may extend but must not contradict them.
integration.dependencyPolicy: Applications consume credential-entry behavior only through public package exports and the canonical Node orchestrator helper.
integration.sdkPolicy: The shared runtime prepares the global TokenManager before generated IAM app SDK calls; consumers do not build raw HTTP requests or manual auth headers.
integration.languagePolicy: Node orchestration and Vite integration share one IAM-owned component boundary and preserve private credential handling.
